title: agoseris glauca var. aspera
intro: Agoseris glauca var. aspera, also known as hairy greasewood or hairy agoseris, is a rare plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Agoseris glauca var. aspera include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically to apply to skin irritations or wounds, or taken internally to boost immune function and reduce inflammation.
